intro: The clearest split between Didit and Pliance is how they charge: Didit uses subscription pricing starting around $666/mo, while Pliance uses custom enterprise pricing (no public tier). Didit is Free KYC verification API with document scanning, NFC chip reading, and facial biometric matching for identity checks; it fits best for Fintech, Marketplace. Pliance is Pliance is a Swedish compliance platform offering APIs for customer due diligence, sanctions and PEP screening, transaction monitoring and ongoing monitoring designed for developers and compliance teams; it fits best for Nordic and EU fintechs wanting modern AML APIs with GDPR-aligned hosting and clear docs. Pick Didit when the job maps to fintech requiring identity verification; pick Pliance when it maps to customer due diligence api for swedish fintech onboarding. Didit is not for consumer apps without regulatory requirements. Pliance is not for us-heavy teams or enterprises needing tier-1 data providers and full case-management workflows. Honest trade-offs: Didit is weak for consumer apps without regulatory requirements, and Pliance is weak for eu-focused, us coverage less competitive — match to your real scenario.
quick_take: Didit is for fintech, marketplace; Pliance is for nordic and eu fintechs wanting modern aml apis with gdpr-ali; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Didit if you want subscription pricing starting around $666/mo and your workload matches fintech, marketplace.
verdict_b: Choose Pliance if you want custom enterprise pricing (no public tier) and your workload matches nordic and eu fintechs wanting modern aml apis with gdpr-aligned hosting and clear docs.

faq.a: Didit has more transparent pricing; Pliance's cost depends on a sales conversation, so a direct comparison is not possible without a quote. Didit uses subscription pricing starting at $666/mo. Pliance uses custom enterprise pricing with no published rates.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Didit to Pliance?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: Pliance is rated high lock-in (Custom KYC contracts). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data. Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Pliance a good alternative to Didit?
faq.a: Pliance sits in the same category as Didit, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Pliance is best fit for nordic and eu fintechs wanting modern aml apis with gdpr-aligned hosting and clear docs. Note: Pliance is not for us-heavy teams or enterprises needing tier-1 data providers and full case-management workflows.
